Sunny sensed the ground beneath him tremble. Then, the vast vertical plain of the gargantuan gate he was standing on began to move with a powerful shudder — at the same time, the other side of the gate moved, as well.

Seemingly slowly because of its prodigious size, but breathtakingly fast in reality, the gates of the wharf closed. Sunny, who had unwittingly caught a ride on one of its wings, barely managed to keep his balance when the two sides shut with a deafening thunderclap, cutting out the Night Garden's path of retreat.

…Of course, if there was a need to make an escape, the titanic ship could simply ram the gates with the indestructible immensity of its stern, causing mayhem and devastation on an unimaginable scale. But Sunny hoped that it would not come to that.
Nothing happened for a minute or two — outwardly, at least.
Then, Sunny heard the roaring rustle of flowing water echoing between the sides of the living ship and the walls of its berth.
That was the turbulent water inside the wharf draining away through invisible channels. Its level slowly dropped, and the Night Garden lowered with it.
Sunny had expected some mechanism to catch and secure it in place. And there was indeed a mechanism… of sorts.

As he watched in wonder, the wooden interior of the colossal wharf blossomed with numerous flowers and leaves. Then, enormous branches stretched outward, gently catching the Night Garden and fusing with its hull.

Soon enough, the water was entirely gone, revealing the floor of the berth. The Night Garden was resting on it, supported by the colossal branches that prevented it from tilting to the side... like a tree that returned to the forest.
The branches, however, were not there simply for support.

As Sunny watched, his eyes widening a little, the powerful currents of soul essence he had observed before flowed through them into the living ship, nourishing it. There seemed to be some kind of sorcery at work, guiding them in fleeting patterns and dictating what effect they had on the weathered expanse of the Night Garden.

The wharf was not merely replenishing its reserves of essence. Under its nurturing influence, the scars marring the hull of the living ship began to close, and countless damaged parts hidden in its cavernous interior began to be mended.

Although Sunny could not perceive it, he was sure that the process of restoration and rejuvenation was happening to the mystical part of the Night Garden, too — the very sorcery that made it function.

So, in the end, he and his companions did not have to learn how to operate the wharf of the Eternal City, or what to do to repair the living ship. Just like the Night Garden, the wharf itself was a living thing — and so, it could mend the titanic vessel all by itself.
The process would probably take quite a while to complete… but it was having an effect already.
Sunny let out a relieved sigh.
'That's one thing I don't need to worry about anymore.'

In fact, the restoration of the Night Garden alone was enough to make this expedition worth it. It was one of the few Great Citadels of humanity, after all, and one that offered the Human Domain a unique and vital advantage, not to mention serving as home for millions of people.
But, of course…
Sunny was not going to be satisfied with just that.

As the process of restoration began, his gaze left the vast expanse of the Night Garden and traveled to the far side of the wharf, where a bridge connecting it to the rest of the Eternal City was hidden.
Somewhere out there, the Dutchman was already leading its army of wraiths to conquer the sunken city. The mysterious third contender was on the move, as well.
The lineage of Storm God waited to be claimed.
…The fragment of Weaver's forbidden lineage did, as well.
And countless treasures of the immortal abode on top of that.
Exhaling slowly, Sunny closed his eyes for a moment and smiled.
"Let's conquer hell, then."
He paused for a few moments, then added in a less solemn tone:
"Uh… one more hell, I mean."

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2627.txt
content: By the time Sunny reached the other side of the blossoming berth, Jet was already there, clad in her fitting black armor and leaning on her scythe. Her icy cold eyes peered at the long bridge connecting the wharf to the Eternal City, radiating a sense of deadly cold.
Sunny appeared out of the shadows next to her, looking in the same direction.
"Where are Naeve, Aether, and Bloodwave?"
She glanced at him briefly.

"Guarding the ship. We stationed all available Awakened throughout the interior, with Masters spread out to defend the key areas. The Saints are protecting the surface decks — Aether is on the bridge, Naeve is at the bow, while Bloodwave is on the stern. The three of them should be able to contend against anything that might get past us… or at least stall it long enough for you to arrive."
Sunny nodded, burning a bit of his essence to substitute the lacking oxygen with it.
"How is their breathing?"
Jet smiled faintly.

"They are fine while aboard the Night Garden. Beyond its aegis… they'll probably suffocate. However, these are Saints of Night we are talking about — they have Memories to deal with the lack of breathing air in spades. So you don't have to worry about them."
Sunny studied her for a moment.
"What about you?"
Jet gave him a stare.
"I am dead. What do I need air for?"
Sunny coughed.

Jet usually breathed like any normal human — when she was not low on essence, at least — and her body metabolized oxygen like any other body would. However, it seemed that her special constitution gave her an alternative, just like Sunny's Supreme Rank did.
There was enough oxygen in the air around them to speak normally, at least. That was a pleasant surprise.
She raised an eyebrow.
"So, what's the plan?"
Sunny studied the bridge for a while, then pointed to a barely visible area of rippling air on the other side of it.
"Do you see that? Let's take a look first, then decide what our next steps will be."
Summoning the Jade Mantle, he reached into the shadows and pulled a black odachi out of it. Then, placing its blade on his shoulder, Sunny walked toward the Eternal City with confident steps.
Jet followed.
As they walked across the long bridge, black water reeled and raged below them, glistening with the reflections of the silver light.

It was still eerily quiet and peaceful around them, though, with no movement whatsoever. Sunny had expected to be carving a path through a sea of abominations by now, but surprisingly enough, nothing had attacked them yet.
His shadow sense also had no trouble enveloping everything around him… up to the end of the bridge, that was. There, his perception grew vague and muddied, as if something was suppressing it.
'Strange…'
Sunny frowned.
"What is that?"
At some point, the smooth stone surface beneath their feet became uneven. Crouching, he wiped away droplets of water and studied it closely.
Thin lines were carved into the bridge, following a strange pattern. After inspecting them for a while, Sunny raised an eyebrow.
"A… runic array?"

Indeed, there were countless runes carved into the stone bridge, forming a complicated enchantment. Sunny was not as knowledgeable about runic sorcery as Cassie was, but he did know the basics. There was no need to test his modest mastery, though, since he could simply ask the master.
[Cassie?]
They continued on their way, observing the endless weave of mysterious runes. Eventually, Cassie saw enough to offer her opinion:
[This is an auxiliary enchantment… way beyond what I could accomplish, but still only a small part of a larger array.]
Sunny tilted his head a little.
[What does it do?]
There was a long pause, and then Cassie's voice resounded in his mind. It was quiet and distorted, as if reaching him from a great distance, but still discernible.

[It maintains the existence of these runes, as well as of the main enchantment that is situated somewhere else. Well… rather, it prevents the innate sorcery of the Eternal City from erasing the runes by restoring the surfaces they are carved into to a pristine condition.]
Sunny narrowed his eyes.
[What does the main enchantment do, then?]
Cassie's quiet, melodious laughter resounded in his head.
[That I do not know. I would have to read it first, wouldn't I?]
Sunny sighed.
By then, they had almost reached the end of the bridge. Stopping, he remained motionless for a bit and then raised his head to study the anomaly they had noticed from the walls of the wharf.
Jet let out a whistle.
"I'll be damned."
Sunny tended to agree.
There, in front of them, a subtle rippling of the air betrayed the existence of an invisible barrier…

There was another dome inside the aegis protecting the Eternal City, covering most of the city. It was very much like the shadow dome he had created to see if the Night Garden could submerge into the water — almost as vast as the primary one, but not quite, acting as a second layer of protection.

Here, the foundation of the Eternal City was exposed, towering above the restless waters as dark cliffs. And on those cliffs… enormous runes were carved into the stone, extending in both directions as far as the eye could see.
Almost as if they encircled the entire city.
There was something quite familiar about how those runes were carved into the base of the Eternal City, as well…
The way they looked was almost exactly the same as Daeron's message on the great stele looked, only on a much larger scale.
[This is the main enchantment. I think I know what its purpose is…]
Sunny remained silent for a long while, looking at the rippling barrier of the secondary dome.
He smiled darkly.
"I think I know, as well."
Jet gave him a questioning look.
Looking up at the endless mass of water looming over their heads, Sunny said:
"The first barrier is created by innate sorcery of the Eternal City… divine sorcery of the Demon of Repose. Its purpose is to keep things out."
Then, he looked down at the great runes carved into the ancient stone.
"The second barrier is a complicated runic array — much like the runic array that protected Twilight. It was created by the Serpent King."
His expression darkened.
"And its purpose… is to keep things in."

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2628.txt
content: Jet studied the wall of subtly rippling air in front of them and the pristine streets beyond it, then rested her scythe on her shoulders in a relaxed pose.
"Well, that explains why nothing has attacked us yet."
She approached the barrier created by the Serpent King, stopping only a few centimeters away from it, then took a few steps along its nearly invisible surface.
"Seems like he was really wary of what hides inside this city, huh?"
Sunny shrugged.
"I guess."
Jet remained silent for a few moments, then asked in a calm tone:
"More importantly, if this barrier prevents things from escaping the Eternal City… wouldn't we get trapped if we pass through it, as well?"
That was what Sunny was thinking about, too.
He frowned.
"Nightwalker escaped it before, so there must be a way. Or maybe a condition sorting those who can leave, and those who can't."
He sighed and shook his head.

"It does not matter, anyway. Anything that Daeron created, I can destroy. It might take some time, though, if we do need to destroy this barrier in order to escape… not to mention that I would not feel right getting rid of it before whatever he was wary of is dealt with, or at least contained in some way."
Jet looked at him with dark amusement.
"Oh? What makes you so confident in your ability?"
Sunny simply shrugged.
"The fact that destroying something is always easier than creating it."

Both Daeron and Sunny were Supreme, and both were powerful sorcerers. So, with all other things being equal, he was confident in his ability to dismantle the runic array Daeron had created — given time.
Jet looked back at the invisible barrier.
"I guess we are going inside, then."
Sunny grimaced.
"Well, yes. But first, we might want to… w—wait, what are you doing?!"
Before he could even finish the sentence, Jet took a step forward and passed through the barrier. Then, she turned around and looked at him with a smile.
"What?"
Sunny stared at her, aghast.
"What do you mean, what?! How could you just go and enter a mysterious magic barrier designed to trap living beings for all eternity?!"
She shrugged.
"Didn't you say that you can easily destroy it?"
Sunny suppressed the impulse to grab his head.
"I say a lot of things!"
She laughed.
"That you do."
She fell silent, studied the surrounding buildings for a few moments, then sighed and took a step back.
Sunny tensed, but Jet seemed to pass through the barrier and return to the bridge with no issue.
Still resting her arms on the shaft of the scythe, she rolled her shoulders and shook her head.

"My guess would be that the Serpent King was unwilling to let the immortals who had populated the Eternal City escape into the Twilight Sea. After all, there is no worse combination than immortality and Corruption. So, this barrier probably prevents anything that died and was restored by the city from leaving its boundary."
Sunny took a few deep breaths.
"...What if your theory turned out to be wrong?"
Jet shrugged.

"Then I would have asked you to get me out. Better yet, I'd ask Naeve to aim those scary cannons you so graciously crafted for me at the runes carved into the foundation of this place and blast a few dozen of them off the face of the earth. I am pretty sure that would have dealt with this runic array, too."
Sunny sighed.
"...I guess that works."

With that, he walked past Jet and entered the barrier, too. As he passed it, an unpleasant feeling momentarily overwhelmed his senses — it was as if something cold and unfeeling was inspecting the depths of his being, searching for an unknown sign.
Then, it brushed against Weaver's lineage and recoiled, retreating in fright.

Sunny found himself standing near the mouth of a wide street. On both sides of him, ornate buildings rose into the darkness, with black water glistening with reflections of silver light as it rolled above him where the sky was supposed to be.
The street was clean and empty, with beautiful sculptures lining the facades of the nearest buildings.
Jet crossed the barrier for the second time and looked around.
"It doesn't seem so bad, actually."
Sunny took a few steps forward, then frowned and looked at the entrance of one of the buildings.
His shadow sense was still somewhat suppressed, but he could feel a presence there.
"Come out."
There were a few moments of silence, and then he heard the sound of light steps.

A young man walked out of the buildings, walking toward them unhurriedly. At first glance, he was just barely past the age when one would become a Sleeper, wearing a free tunic woven out of flowing, luxurious fabric. His features were soft and harmonious, beautiful enough to mistake him for a Saint, and his glistening hair was held in place by an exquisitely decorated band of silver.
He did not seem armed, and a faint smile was playing on his lips. Walking to the middle of the street, the beautiful youth stopped and turned, looking at them with a strange, curious expression.
Jet quietly removed her scythe from her shoulders, grasping it tightly.
"Oh, hell…"
Sunny almost took a step back.
Because when he peered into the soul of the striking young man, what met him… was a vast ocean of vile, boundless darkness.
The youth opened his mouth, as if wishing to welcome them to the Eternal City…
What escaped his mouth, however, were no words.
Instead, it was an avalanche of grotesque, pulsating, hungry flesh.

A dozen long tendrils ending with sharp spikes shot toward Sunny with such speed that he barely had time to react. Still, he managed to put his odachi in the path — only to feel it shatter from the impact, not offering the fleshy tendrils any resistance.
A split second later, the spikes were about to pierce his head.

Sunny dissolved into shadows, leaving them whipping furiously through the air. The stone plates of the wide street exploded into a hurricane of sharp shards, and the nearest facade collapsed into a cloud of dust when one of the tendrils brushed against it in passing.
Jet's scythe flashed, severing another. She moved with graceful ease, dodging the deadly attack by a hair's breadth.

In the next moment, Sunny stepped from the shadows behind the monstrous youth and calmly beheaded him with the reformed blade of his odachi. Red blood spluttered on the stone rubble, and the corpse of the young man fell to his knees while his head rolled a few steps, dragging the limp tentacles behind it.
"...Curses."
Sunny watched as the headless corpse slowly toppled to the ground.
Jet did not relax, glancing at him briefly.
"What's with that expression?"
He hesitated for a moment.
"It's because…"
There was no new shade in his Soul Sea.
"...I don't think that thing is dead."
Just as he said those words, the headless body stirred and floated into an upright position.
The grotesque tendrils protruding from the mouth of the severed head, meanwhile, twitched and moved, raising it a dozen meters above the ground like long, abominable legs.
The youth's eyes peered at Sunny with the same innocent, mild curiosity.
He shivered.
'Damnation…'

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2629.txt
content: There were deadly beings out there, in the dreadful reaches of the ruthless and broken world… and then there was the Lord of Shadows, Sunless — the Sovereign of Death.

Among those who were just one step behind him, Soul Reaper Jet was perhaps the most lethal. She was definitely the most feared, and for a good reason. Few could survive her insidious Aspect and deathly resolve.
So, it was no exaggeration to say that when the two of them decided to fight side by side, there was hardly a more deadly duo than the Lord of Shadows and Soul Reaper.
…And yet, they failed to kill the eerie abomination that had welcomed them to the Eternal City.

The battle was swift, but furious. Several blocks of the sunken city had been reduced to rubble as Sunny and Jet fought the monstrous youth… or, rather, the grotesque abomination that wore the exquisite face of a human youth. The ancient buildings toppled, and the stone canvas of the roads exploded into an avalanche of jagged shards.
The silence of the empty streets was vanquished by the roaring clamor of a terrifying battle, and the beautiful landscape of the ancient city was replaced by a scene of total devastation.
'Curse it.'

Sunny dashed back, barely evading another fleshy tentacle. The breathtaking statue he had hidden behind was split in two, severed by the spike at the end of the tendril so cleanly that the cut seemed like no more than a thin crack for a few moments.
"This… is a bit tough…"

Jet had spun her ghostly scythe, slicing several tendrils, then allowed the Mist Blade to assume the form of a graceful lucerne and used its long spike to pin the nearest severed tentacle to the cobblestones. It was just in time — despite being separated from the main body of the abomination, the fleshy tentacles continued to move, trying to slither across the rubble toward her.
"What the hell do we do?"
Sunny cursed again.

The eldritch horror was strong — about as strong as a Great Beast would be, making it a walking calamity. Even a Supreme like him had to stay alert in a battle with this thing, so it was understandable that Jet was struggling.

However, that was no reason for them to be having so much trouble dealing with the grotesque abomination. Sunny could slay a Great Beast in a heartbeat, and Jet was more than formidable enough to bridge the gap between the Ranks.
By all accounts, the eerie creature should have been dead by now.
And in fact, they had already killed it many times…
It was just that the grotesque abomination refused to stay dead.
Sunny and Jet had cut, pierced, and crushed its body. They dealt fatal damage to its soul and poisoned it with the Death Will. The creature was burned to death and shattered after being frozen solid.

But no matter what they did, the beautiful youth was restored to his original appearance before too long. Its crushed, severed, burned, shattered body returned to a flawless state, and then turned hideous and horrid once again as it swelled with malformed flesh and burst into a slithering forest of deadly tendrils.
It did not take a wise man to realize what was happening.
'Damn... that damn daemon!'

The fiend they were fighting was powerful, but in and of itself, it was not too terrifying. As evidence, Sunny and Jet were still unscathed despite battling it for several minutes already. The problem, however, was that it was immortal — or rather, that the Eternal City made it immortal.
The distinction was subtle, but important.

The incredible, inexhaustible vitality that the monstrous youth possessed was not his own power. Rather, it was the manifestation of the sorcery that governed this cursed place — and so, killing him was not a matter of overpowering a Great Beast.
It was a matter of overcoming the sorcery of a daemon, and while Sunny had a high opinion of his prowess, that was not a feat that he could accomplish just yet.
How did one fight an immortal abomination, then?
Dodging another explosion of hungry flesh, Sunny could not help but smile darkly.
'No, but it's quite ironic, isn't it?'
Two dead warriors were trying to kill an undying monster.
Brandishing his black odachi, he called out to Jet:
"We can't kill it!"
His gaze landed on the shambling, appalling figure of the corrupted immortal.
"So, let's subdue it instead!"

He lunged forward, turning into a torrent of shadows to evade the whipping tendrils, and manifested into a corporeal form right in front of the abomination. His odachi flashed, severing the creature's arms — at the same time, a current of mist coalesced into a lithe figure behind the immortal, and Jet used her scythe to cut his legs.

As the fiend fell to the ground, dozens of black chains burst from it and wrapped themselves around him. Individually, none of them were strong enough to bind him, but all together, they held long enough for more chains to descend on the struggling form and bury it under the mass of inky-black metal.
Soon, there was nothing but a small hill of black links towering where the horrid youth once stood, trembling slightly like a macabre cocoon.
Sunny stared at it somberly.
A large swath of the Eternal City around them was entirely decimated…
But the ornate buildings were already restoring themselves, too.
Shattered statues coalesced into their original, elegant forms. Toppled pillars rose from the rubble and assembled themselves. The rubble floated into the air and returned to its previous shape.
The buildings grew from the devastated ruin like flowers, and the paving of the street smoothed itself out.

Before too long, Sunny and Jet found themselves standing on a beautiful plaza. Their surroundings were spotless and undamaged, with only the cocoon of black chains and their heavy breathing proving that the battle had happened.
Jet stared at the small hill of chains and then looked at Sunny with a somber expression.
"Hey."
He raised an eyebrow.
"What?"
She pointed to the black cocoon with the blade of her war scythe.
"That was just one of them, right?"
Sunny nodded slowly, prompting her to give him a dark smile.
"...And you said that there were thousands, or maybe even millions of these things within the city?"
He hesitated for a few moments, suppressing a shiver.
What could he say?
Sunny sighed.
"Sounds about right…"
As the person who had invited Jet to this nightmarish place, he was starting to feel a tiny bit guilty.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2630.txt
content: Sunny did not want to admit it, but he was troubled.
The battle had been intense, and the enemy was powerful… but that was not the reason for his unease.
The fact that the abomination had turned out to be immortal wasn't, either.
Even the knowledge that there was a vast horde of these undying fiends populating the Eternal City was not the reason why he felt so somber.
No…
The real reason why Sunny was troubled was that he could not make sense of the monstrous youth.
There was something quite strange about this Nightmare Creature, to the point that he was not even sure if he was correct to call it one.

First, Sunny did not see what he usually saw when peering into the souls of abominations — there was the vile darkness of Corruption, true, but it did not possess the usual nodes that served as sources of the spreading, tumorous infection. So, he could not even guess the Class of the creature, only tell that it was roughly of the Great Rank.
Second, even Cassie could not tell what the horrid fiend was. All she could say after peering at its essence was a few cryptic words:
[It is… like a part of a greater whole hiding inside an usurped vessel.]
Then, after falling silent for a while, she added in a hesitant tone:
[But the greater whole it is a part of is itself incomplete, longing to be mended.]
The fact that Cassie's Dormant Ability could not draw knowledge about the eldritch horror was already a cause for concern. Her words describing what little she did glimpse were even more worrisome.
'A part of a greater whole, usurped vessel, incomplete…'
What the hell did it mean?
And what the hell was the corrupted immortal, really?
Sunny sighed.

The cocoon of black chains continued to stir subtly, since the horrid fiend was still struggling to break free. Sunny could keep it bound without too much trouble… but he could only do so by maintaining the manifested shadows personally. Making the chains permanent would weaken them, which would allow the monstrous youth to escape.
Which meant that while he could contain this particular abomination — or maybe even a thousand of them — he could not contain them all. He could not even contain most of them, which was a problem.
He grimaced and looked at the black underside of the rolling sea above them, with silver light reflecting from its restless surface.
Suddenly, it occurred to him that this was a kind of black sky, as well.
"Ah… this is going to be quite a spectacle."
Jet wore a somber expression, her icy gaze hiding a hint of some dark emotion.
She lingered for a while, glanced at her scythe briefly, and then said in a neutral tone:
"We can still retreat, you know."
Sunny slowly shook his head.
"No. There's nowhere to retreat."
He looked away from the pile of black chains and turned to her.

"It might seem like we can escape to safer waters, but that is an illusion. There is nowhere safe in this world… in two worlds, even. Anywhere and everywhere can and will turn into a worse hell. All of existence is a warzone, so if we can't deal even with this much, then what's the point of seeking safety somewhere else?"
Jet let out a hollow chuckle.
"This much is a city full of immortal Great Ones, though. With an army of wraiths added as a free bonus. So what are we going to do?"
Sunny studied her for a while, then smiled darkly.
"For now… I suggest you take a few steps back."
Jet frowned.
"Why? What are you going to do?"
Sunny looked around, noting that he could not see the wharf from behind the tall buildings.
"Let me show you. This should be far enough, I think."
Saying that, he reached into his soul…
And summoned something.

Not long before that, Aiko made her way to the throne room of the Dark Castle, carrying the latest report. Her boss was lounging on his throne, as usual, looking into the distance with an amused expression.
She yawned, then belatedly covered her mouth with her dainty hand.
At the moment, Aiko really missed her opulent bedroom on the highest floor of the castle.
"Hey, boss. About our investigation into the strange trends on the Black Market…"
He waved his hand lazily, interrupting her, and then looked at her with a sly smile.
'Uh-oh.'
Suddenly, Aiko had a very, very bad feeling.
Sunny seemed inordinately happy. Why was he so happy?!
"Hey, Aiko. Let me ask you a question. How would you go about conquering a city full of immortal Nightmare Creatures?"
She frowned.
"I wouldn't. I'd turn around and run away. No, actually, I would never end up anywhere near such a dreadful place, to begin with. Only a complete, irremediable idiot would. No, really."
Her boss blinked a few times with an offended expression on his pale face.
"Hey! That's harsh!"
Aiko suppressed the desire to palm her face.
"Wait, that was not a hypothetical question? What the… where the hell did you find a city full of immortal abominations?! And why?! Why would you do something like that?!"
Sunny raised his chin.
"No, it wasn't. Under the sea. And… why, because there's treasure involved!"
Aiko was about to say more, but froze instead.
A moment later, her eyes glistened.
Her expression changed subtly.
"Treasure? Huh. What kind of…"
But her boss shook his head.
"Nope. Since you're apparently full of prejudice against immortal abominations, I am not taking you with me. Not a chance. Don't even ask!"
She just stared at him for a moment.
"I wasn't going to, though?"
Her boss nodded.
"Great. Then… if you really don't want to go, I suggest you vacate the premises. Fast."
Aiko stared at him in confusion.
At that moment, something eerie happened.
Three more versions of Sunny appeared from the shadows behind her, walking toward the throne.
"And I mean really fast."
"Immediately would be best."
"You can set up a temporary office in the Nameless Temple. There's a cot in the inner sanctum, I think? Oh, and don't forget to water my tree!"
'This is bad…'
The various incarnations of her boss very rarely gathered in one place.
And every time they did, something terrible happened.
"Oh, no!"
Not wasting any time, Aiko dashed forward, sprinted past the throne… and jumped out of the window.
As she hovered in the lightless sky of the Forgotten Shorer, looking down at the magnificent edifice of the Dark Castle…
The castle suddenly vanished without a trace.
'Oh, no! My bedroom!'
And just a few moments later, it appeared again somewhere else.
To be precise, it appeared on an empty, pristine plaza in the Eternal City.
Jet had barely managed to take a few steps back before the black spires of the Dark Castle suddenly rose from the shadows enveloping the square.
Soon enough, a magnificent black fortress appeared out of nowhere between the ornate buildings, casting a deep shadow upon them.
Then, its gates opened with a chilling groan…
And out of the darkness inside, a vast legion of silent shades marched onto the ancient streets.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2631.txt
content: Sunny pulled Jet into the shadows, bringing them both to stand on the tall walls of the Dark Castle.

Below them, an endless river of darkness was flowing out of its gates, slowly consuming the wide open expanse of the magnificent plaza. That was his Shadow Legion — every shade he had collected over the years, except for a few left to guard his Citadel in the Burned Forest and the Sacred shades he could not summon on a whim.

Men and Nightmare Creatures, united in the tranquil embrace of his deathly authority — those who came from the Forgotten Shore, the Chained Isles, from the cold reaches of Antarctica, the endless tides of the Great River, the sweltering heat of Godgrave, the ash and cinders of the Burned Forest, and many places beyond those.
All of them had come to the Eternal City now, assembling into a vast battle formation in utter silence — hundreds of thousands of dead warriors, all ready to wage war on the immortal swarm.
Some flooded the plaza below, some manned the walls of the Dark Castle. The castle itself trembled as it stirred, casting a hungry gaze at the beautiful streets beyond.
But that was not all.
From the shadows to Sunny's left, Saint rose in the fearsome glory of her black armor. From the shadows to his right, Slayer appeared like a ghost.

Far below, a towering figure forged from blackened steel and infernal flames stepped from the castle gates, the silver light of the distant spires reflecting dimly from the countless jagged blades that littered its impenetrable carapace.
A colossal serpent with onyx scales wrapped itself around one of the towers of the Dark Castle, letting out a chilling hiss that washed over the world as the war horn of Death.
A tenebrous steed with flames burning in its crimson eyes ascended the wall to stand beside its master, the ringing of its adamantine hooves echoing across the vast plaza.
And, finally…
The shadows stirred and rose, coalescing into four dark figures.
When they appeared, the light of the silver spires seemed to dim, and the abyssal darkness pressing down on the Eternal City seemed to grow even darker.
A cold wind blew across the plaza, bringing with it the promise of murder and death.
Those four had the same face as Sunny, each wielding the power of a Supreme being.
That was the Lord of Shadows and his legion — an insidious and indestructible force capable of washing over entire realms and reducing them to rubble.
Now, that force had invaded the sunken city of the Demon of Repose.
Standing by his side, Jet sighed quietly.
"Wow."
She remained motionless for a few moments, observing the plaza.

Where there had been only emptiness minutes ago now stood a fearsome castle. Beyond its walls, a vast army of silent shadows was ready for battle. The very atmosphere of the Eternal City had changed, announcing the arrival of a new, terrifying invader.
Jet studied Sunny carefully, then shook her head.
"So this is the power of a Sovereign…"

She had witnessed Nephis descend into battle, but not the Sovereign of Death. Beholding his Supreme power felt different, though — perhaps because the visible manifestation of Changing Star's power, despite its unimaginable destructiveness, was personal in nature. This, however, illustrated the essence of what a Sovereign was far more demonstrably.
A being who wielded authority as a weapon, whose influence spread over the world and subjugated multitudes…
A ruler.
It was strange to think that she was acquainted with a creature like that.
Sunny gave her a confused look.
"Oh, right. You haven't really seen me get serious, have you?"

In Jet's defense, no one had really seen the Lord of Shadow wield his Supreme power. The Shadow Legion had mostly been confined to the Burned Forest ever since Sunny became a Sovereign, and he was the only human there. Cassie must have witnessed how tyrannical his Domain was, but mostly, people had only glimpsed it during that last, fateful battle in Godgrave.
Sunny had just barely attained Supremacy back then, though, so it hardly counted as a conscious expression of his true strength.
Jet shook her head again.

"No. And, how do I put it… strangely enough, you don't give an impression of someone who wields this kind of terrifying power. To be honest, it is hard to remember that you are a Sovereign when you're around."
Sunny grinned.
"Really? Why, thank you! That is by design."
Jet raised an eyebrow, prompting him to shrug.

"Nobody said that a Sovereign has to emanate a solemn and unapproachable aura of suffocating dominance, you know? All that zeal and worship Nephis is subjected to… ugh, I find it weird. No, thank you. There's a more practical reason why I prefer to act as a fun and easygoing demigod, as well."
It was because he could not allow anyone to worship him, since doing so would bring the Forgotten God closer to awakening.
Sunny sighed.
"Oh, and by the way. There is a reason why I summoned my castle and my army now… and it's not just to make an awesome entrance."
Jet smiled faintly.
"Let me guess — it's because thousands of these corrupted immortals are already rushing at us."
Sunny was surprised.
"How did you know?"
She looked at the Shadow Legion.
"Because that is a defensive formation, and the shadows on the walls of the castle are all taking positions as if expecting a siege."
Sunny remained silent for a few moments, then grimaced.

"You are right. I can't sense our surroundings very well, but I do feel them approaching — more or less every abomination that has been dwelling on this particular island of the Eternal City. The first ones will arrive in under a minute, I'd say."
He glanced down at the cocoon of black chains, which was contained in the courtyard of the Dark Castle now.
"So, we have around a minute to decide how to win a battle against these things…"

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2632.txt
content: Looking at the vast army of silent shades arrayed on the plaza below them, Sunny inhaled deeply.

There wasn't much time for them to assess the situation and prepare for battle… but then again, this particular battle was merely the prelude for the frightening war that would swallow the Eternal City soon.
He exhaled.
"Actually, I am not too concerned about this first skirmish. You should probably understand why."

Jet was an experienced commander, after all — in fact, she was far more experienced than he was, in that regard. She had already been a high-ranking officer during the Southern Campaign, having commanded various government forces throughout her storied career — taking charge of the entire First Irregular Company was merely the next step.

Later, Sunny had mostly faced the perils of the Dream Realm on his own, while Jet's responsibilities grew exponentially. He did take charge of a contingent of the Sword Army in Godgrave, but that paled in comparison to her leadership experience, both civil and military. There was little difference between the two in the Age of Nightmares, anyway.
So, he could hardly find a better advisor.
She nodded.

"The Eternal City is vast, and it is separated into islands by the powerful currents of water. We have just arrived; so, the abominations we will face are merely those of them who happened to be on this particular island when we landed."
Her expression darkened.

"The other inhabitants of the Eternal City would have already sensed our presence, though. Even if they have not, the clamor of this battle will reveal our location to them. So, we will be beset by many times more enemies before too long… and they will come at us from various directions, threatening to outflank our forces or bypass them entirely to reach the Night Garden."
Sunny smiled faintly.
"So, what do you recommend we do?"
Jet fell silent for a few moments, contemplating. Then, she said in a somber tone:

"The situation is unusual, because apart from the current rulers of the city, there is not one, but two invading forces. The terrain is difficult, as well… so, although I would have suggested that we entrench ourselves and let the enemy crush themselves into pulp against our defences, that is not an option now."
She looked into the distance, calculating.

"We will need to advance, conquer territory, and play a strategic game of cat and mouse with these creepy immortals. Most importantly, we will need to keep advancing at a sufficient rate and achieve a relatively swift victory. There are two possible outcomes if we don't, and neither of them promises a good ending for us."
Sunny's smile turned a little dark.
He nodded.

"Indeed. If the Dutchman is wiped out, the immortals who are currently tied up fighting against the wraith army will all descend upon us, doubling the already overwhelming strength of our enemies. If it is the opposite and the wraiths conquer the city, though… gods, who knows what terrible fate will befall us? They will get to all the treasures we are aiming for first, at the very least. And something tells me that they'll also grow stronger as a result."
He glanced in the direction where a towering lighthouse was shining with radiant light, far in the distance, and sighed.
"So, this dreadful race seems to be far from over. We are still running against the clock."
It was in more senses than one, as well.
Sunny and Jet had only discussed the situation briefly, but the time afforded to them was already almost over.
"These are issues of strategy, though."
Saying those words, Jet gave him an evaluating look.
"What we need to discuss first is tactics. How do we even battle something that cannot be killed?"
That was the issue, indeed.
Sunny looked down at the black cocoon of shadow chains. He remained silent for a few moments, then said evenly:

"Actually, my shades cannot be killed, either. Not through usual means, at least — even if they are destroyed, they will eventually come back. It is just that they need some time to mend themselves. The immortals of the Eternal City take time to be revived, as well, although it seems much shorter."
His expression darkened.

"In that short moment between destruction and restoration, though, they are vulnerable — that is when we can contain them. As long as we come up with a reliable manner of containment, that is. So… at the end, it all comes down to simple math. We need to disable them faster than they vanquish my shades."
Sensing movement near the entrances to the plaza, Sunny sighed.

"Which means that there would be no decisive victory or defeat in this conflict. Instead, it's going to be a war of attrition. What we must do is leverage our advantages and employ strategies to maximize the enemy losses while minimizing our own. Then, we will be able to conquer the Eternal City instead of being devoured by it."
Jet flexed, stretching her shoulders, and gave him an amused smile.
"That was my conclusion, as well. As for the details…"
She looked at the far side of the plaza, where a wide road and countless smaller alleys opened into it from the depths of the city.
"Well, I guess we will have to iron them out as we go."
Her tone turned a little somber — because there, in the distance, a small silhouette appeared at the mouth of a dark alley.
Then, there was another, and another, and another.

Soon enough, a crowd had gathered at the edge of the plaza — as if the citizens of the Eternal City had to come to peruse the goods of an open-air market. All of them were young and breathtakingly beautiful, wearing light and elegant garments, their smooth faces and glistening eyes betraying no sign of the hidden Corruption.
None of them was armed, either…
And yet, their placid, curious glances made Sunny shiver.
He took a deep breath and then gazed at the distant immortals coldly.
"Let's do that, then. The devil hides in the details, after all…"

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2633.txt
content: 'How ironic.'

The immortals stood at the boundary of the vast plaza, their beautiful and youthful figures drowning in the stark darkness cast by the tall buildings of the Eternal City. Only their eyes glistened like cold silver stars, reflecting the light of the magnificent spires.
They seemed to be studying the Shadow Legion with curious smiles… but that was an illusion. By now, Sunny knew that their smiles were merely masks that these abominations wore to resemble humans.
There was nothing human about them, though.

Now that he could see them clearer, he noticed things that had escaped him during the frenetic battle with the fiend who had welcomed them to the city. Something moved under their supple skin from time to time, as if straining to escape, and their breathtaking faces turned monstrous for a split second now and then.
Before, Sunny had assumed that these abominations took human shape to hide their harrowing nature. But now, he realized that they looked human through no effort or will of their own.

On the contrary, the beautiful immortals were endlessly straining to become monsters. It was just that every time their bodies began to mutate into grotesque abominations, the same sorcery that granted them endless life returned their flesh to its original form.
So, they were not fiends who pretended to be humans. Instead, they were monsters trapped in human bodies against their will…
For all eternity.
This eerie contradiction made his skin crawl, and considering that the skin of these fallen immortals literally crawled, that only disturbed Sunny more.
While it seemed like the beautiful youths were observing the Shadow Legion with curiosity, in fact, they were simply waiting for more of them to arrive.
A few figures turned into a crowd, and a crowd turned into a swarm.
The swarm flowed onto the plaza like a tide.
Sunny inhaled deeply.
'Here we go.'
His mind spun like a wheel.
What was the best way to fight these monsters? How could he achieve victory, and do it in a way that was the most beneficial for him and the Shadow Legion?
Sunny considered what he needed to do for a brief moment and quickly came up with an answer.
Funnily enough, it was almost offensively simple.

At the beginning of his journey as an Awakened, Sunny had mostly relied on his skill and combat intelligence to survive. His technique, physical prowess, and state of mind had decided the outcome of battles.

Later, however, things changed. As both Sunny and his enemies grew more powerful, most of his victories were earned by his wit and his soul arsenal instead. Skill and martial prowess were still of vital importance, of course, serving as the irreplaceable foundation of everything, but he won countless battles by using obscure enchantments of inconspicuous Memories in unexpected ways.

The outcome of battles he fought these days, meanwhile, was often decided by more mystical forces. Who was stronger, whose power was more versatile? Who knew how to use it best? In the clashes of deities, the very concepts of what they were often played the most important role.
Facing the swarm of immortal abominations, though, Sunny realized that he had made a full circle.

Since the conquest of the Eternal City would come down to a battle of attrition, Sunny had to minimize his losses. And to minimize his losses, he had to adhere to a wholly primitive set of tactical values.

There was no trick to overcoming this trial, just mundane warfare. The greatest advantage Sunny wielded was that his Shadow Legion was an organized combat force, while the fallen immortals, while dreadfully powerful, were merely a monstrous mob. So, he had to make sure that his shades fought with the skill and technique of exalted warriors, and that all of them displayed flawless cohesion.

Beyond that, the tried and tested rules of military strategy would be of utmost importance. He had to prevent the enemy from flanking his army, for example, and use terrain to his advantage. Whenever possible, he would have to reduce his losses by building or usurping fortifications. Even the simplest elements of warfare, like battle formations, would once again become vital.
It was a far cry from the conceptual battles and clashes of opposing Aspects… but it was also familiar territory.
So, Sunny was able to adjust his mentality swiftly.
There were mere moments left before the fallen immortals descended on the battle formation of the Shadow Legion…
And in those few moments, Sunny made a decision.

With the nature of the battle for the Eternal City being what it is, his ability to finely control each of his shades was more important than ever. He had already achieved great success in that regard thanks to Mind Weave, but even that was not enough.
So, taking a deep breath, he summoned one more shade.
The Rat King was resting in the lightless expanse of his soul now… which meant that Sunny could call upon a different Sacred shadow.
As the fallen immortals rushed forward, their bodies rippling and bursting into torrents of eviscerating flesh, a colossal silhouette appeared above the plaza.
Then, a titanic black moth opened its vast wings, perched atop the main tower of the Dark Castle and looming over its walls.
Sunny had summoned the shadow of Puppeteer Moth, the Spirit of Doubt.
As it regarded the plaza with its enormous black eyes, countless strands of ethereal black silk shot forward, spreading through the air like a spiderweb — so thin as to almost be invisible.
Each of those strings buried itself in the back of a shade, connecting them all to the Puppeteer.
At the same time, one of Sunny's incarnations turned into a shadow and wrapped itself around the Sacred shade, sharing its senses.
'Aaah…'
Sunny shuddered, overwhelmed by the feeling of fusing with the shadow of a Sacred being. It was too strange, too vast, too great for him to fathom fully…
But he did fathom the connection between the Puppeteer and the soldiers of the Shadow Legion.
Like that, Sunny gained nearly flawless control of his undying army.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2634.txt
content: The flood of slithering, hungry flesh collided with the vanguard of the Shadow Legion.
A vast swath of the beautiful plaza exploded into a cloud of stone shards, then was restored to pristine condition, then was destroyed again.

The fallen immortals each possessed the power of a Great One, bending the very fabric of the world with their profane will. However, the enemies they faced were not weak, either — there, at the forefront of the great battle formation, the shades of the Black Millipedes and the Ember Queen flowed forward like a dark tide, unleashing their fury upon the abominable fiends.
The millipedes were connected to the Puppeteer through the ethereal strands of black silk, and through the Puppeteer to Sunny.

Suddenly, he could sense and command numerous monstrous, segmented bodies. Sunny had a myriad of sharp legs and a myriad of even sharper mandibles, using them to rip and tear the flesh of the immortal swarm to shreds.
The prisoners of the Eternal City were unmade, and then restored once again.
Countless shades were vanquished, returning into the dark embrace of his soul.

A moment later, the tide of millipedes rippled and changed its behavior. Instead of flowing forward like a tide of chilling, bestial fury, the millipede formed an unbreakable wall from their glossy black chitin. Those of them who had been cast by beings of higher Classes rose above that wall, spitting torrents of corrosive black acid at the advancing immortals…
For a few moments, the plaza was drowned by acrid smoke.
Then, segments of the chitin wall were breached or torn apart, and the carnage continued.
Sunny observed the battle through countless eyes — and through his own four pairs of them, as well.
"I've noticed something peculiar."
Jet's voice was strangely calm despite the terrifying spectacle happening in front of them.
Sunny raised an eyebrow.
"What?"
She looked around. Her gaze lingered on Saint, Slayer, Nightmare, the gargantuan shape of Serpent wrapped around one of the Dark Castle's towers, and the lumbering figure of Fiend far below.
"You seem to be holding back."
Sunny forced out a smile.
"I need to establish a baseline to know how fast my shades can be destroyed by these things. Once I know that, I will know how to plan for future battles better."
She frowned slightly.
"Are the losses you are suffering worth it?"
He grimaced.
"That is something I need to find out, as well."

Sunny was not going to sacrifice too many shades in pursuit of vital information, though. He was definitely not going to sacrifice the Ember Queen and her more powerful champions — so, when the wall of chitin came close to collapsing, he moved one to the next test.
Three of his incarnations stepped through the shadows and fused with him, augmenting his power.
'Ah… feels great.'

This was the strongest he had been after the final battle at Godgrave, so Sunny almost felt like himself again. Enjoying the feeling of ferocious power running through his veins, he cast his attention to something else.
The moment four of his incarnations were reunited, four links of the Curse fused, as well. Instantly, its power soared… and his did, too.
In fact, it worked so well that Sunny was stunned for a brief moment.
'Oh. I see what is happening.'

It was the [Absence of Light] enchantment doing its work. That enchantment was supposed to grant Sunny more power when he was surrounded by deep shadows… and where would he find shadows that were deeper than those that dwelled at the bottom of the Stormsea, in the abyssal darkness that had never been touched even by an echo of sunlight?
Perhaps only in the Shadow Realm.
Spending a split second to come to terms with his immense potency, Sunny inhaled slowly, and then activated two of the three core enchantments of the Curse: [Shadow Flame] and [Mercy of Shadow].
Instantly, the augmentation he was enjoying was transferred to the champion shades of the Black Millipede tribe. At the same time, more of his deathly will was expressed through them, as well.
The cadence of the battle changed.
Far below, the rate at which his shades were being destroyed increased drastically. At the same time, the rate at which the fallen immortals were obliterated increased.
Not that it mattered much, considering that they were revived before too long.
Sunny frowned.

The effect was significant… but that was because there were relatively few of the immortal abominations on the outskirts of the Eternal City. Once they delved deeper into its streets, the scale of the battles would increase, and therefore, the impact of the Curse would become less pronounced.
But that was valuable information in and of itself, because it helped him understand what kind of engagements he had to avoid, and what kind he had to force.
Jet sighed.
"Are you done gathering information, perhaps?"
Sunny lingered for a few moments and then sighed.
"Yes. That should do, for now."
It seemed as if Slayer had been waiting to hear these exact words. A moment after Sunny spoke them, she was already drawing her bow, the tip of her arrow swelling with blinding light.
At the same time, Saint turned into a torrent of darkness and rushed toward the battle. Nightmare leaped into the air, while Serpent slithered to the ground like a river of onyx scales.
Fiend simply plummeted into the shadows, stepping through them to appear just beyond the mass of Shadow Millipedes.
He did not manage to disappear before Sunny stirred and shouted, his voice full of concern:
"Don't eat them!"

The first thing he had thought about when presented with the problem of containing the fallen immortals was feeding them to either Fiend or Mimic. However, the dwellers of the Eternal City were too eerie, too unnerving. Sunny was worried that swallowing one of these creatures would allow it to infect his Sadows.
Observing the Shadow Legion stir, Jet asked in a calm tone:
"So... are we joining the fun, as well?"
Sunny looked at her and smiled.
"Of course. We wouldn't want to miss something so memorable, would we?"

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2635.txt
content: The silent ranks of the Shadow Legion stirred under the walls of the Dark Castle. The cadence of the battle changed as the black tide moved forward, flowing across the shattered ground. The titanic figures of Goliath and the Defiled Seeker lumbered above the black tide of dead soldiers, making the plaza shake with each step; the countless threads of ethereal black silk trembled, forming a vast spiderweb.

The fallen immortals were still engaged in a battle against the Ember Queen and her millipede swarm. Individually, each of them was more powerful than an average shade under Sunny's command… however, in this battle at least, the numerical advantage was on his side.

So, the rulers of the Eternal City swiftly found themselves flanked. Bestial shades of Godgrave had flanked them from the left, while the Shadow Wolves and the Obsidian Wasps had flanked them from the right. At the same time, the shadow of Daeron led the shades of human Saints into a frontal assault to support the Ember Queen.

Clad in the armor forged by the shadow of the King of Swords, the shadow of the King of Twilight descended upon the immortals of the Eternal City like a storm. His polearm carved a bloody path through the mass of slivering flesh, causing the thunderous roars of devastating impacts to roll across the battlefield.

It was somewhat ironic, but in this battle between Great and Supreme beings — those who were only a step away from becoming deities — the mundane laws of strategy were just as important as mystical matters.

The inhabitants of the Eternal City might have been immortals, but they still could not overcome the dire disadvantage of being attacked from the flank. Attacked from several directions at the same time, they began to falter.
A few moments later, a blinding explosion bloomed among the fallen immortals, turning hundreds of them to dust — that was Slayer's arrow finding its mark.

Fiend emerged from the shadows of the battlefield, towering above it like a steel colossus forged in the depths of hell. A plume of red flame escaped from his maw, turning a vast area in front of him into a blazing inferno, and his razor-sharp claws severed countless bodies in half.

Saint rose from a vast torrent of darkness. Despite towering more than ten meters above ground, she moved with her usual grace — the weight of her firm steps, however, was crushing enough to send cracks running through the stone plates covering the plaza.

Serpent plunged into the hideous mass of fallen immortals, rampaging among them like a formless, ever-changing spirit of death. It took whatever shape was most suitable for destroying the enemy, and remained in its own serpentine form when there was no better choice
Nightmare tore their grotesque flesh with his horns, hooves, and fangs...

Jet observed the awesome spectacle of the terrifying battle with a calm gaze. She sighed quietly and then jumped onto the parapet of the Dark Castle's rampart. The wind sent her raven-black hair dancing, and wisps of chilling mist surrounded her lithe figure.
"Have you come up with a way to actually contain these fiends yet?"
Sunny gave her a neutral look.
"...I'm working on it."
She grinned and raised a fist into the air.
"Work hard!"
With that, she turned into mist and disappeared from view, as if dispelled by the wind.
Soon enough, a terrifying wraith of icy mist appeared among the fallen immortals, severing their repulsive bodies with a ghostly scythe.
Sunny let out a satisfied sigh.
"Well then, it's time for me to go, too."
He took a few steps back, lowered his body, and then dashed forward. Racing the parapet of the battlements, he jumped and soared into the sky.
Half a dozen seconds later, Sunny plummeted to the ground like a dark comet, falling into the very middle of the immortal swarm.

His impact made the entire island quake, and a violent explosion tore everything around him to shreds. A devastating shockwave rolled outward, breaking the bodies of undying abominations and sending them to the ground. Those who were near the explosion were simply reduced to ash.

When the dust settled, there was a vast crater in the spot where Sunny had landed, surrounded by a wide, empty area. Standing in the middle of that carnage and watching calmly as the swirling flakes of ash slowly coalesced into human bodies, he smiled.
"See?! I am working on it hard!"
The helmet of the Jade Mantle hid his face, and a black shield wove itself out of shadows on his arm.
"Serpent!"
The gargantuan Shadow slithered his way and rippled, then turned into a black sword that fell into his hand.

Having suffered from how swift and deadly the flesh tendrils of a fallen immortal were, Sunny had abandoned his usual odachi in favor of a one-handed sword and a shield. He was proficient in this form of combat as well, of course, he just did not use it often.
Now that he did, however…
Sunny felt a strong and unexplainable impulse to strike the rim of his shield twice.
'I think I am starting to understand why Saint does it…'
He was surrounded by the immortal fiends by then.
Before, one of them had been enough to make Sunny sweat. Now, there were dozens of them around him — and behind them, hundreds more.
But Sunny was not the same as he had been then.

At the moment, he was augmented by three of his shadows and wielded four links of the Curse on top of that. The Jade Mantle and its [Underworld Armament] enchantment empowered the shadowbound charm, as the [Absence of Light] enchantment of the charm empowered Sunny.
The sword he wielded, meanwhile, was the manifestation of a Supreme Titan — Serpent, his slaying blade.
So, his strength had become more unfathomably ferocious, making it feel like the world was straining to contain him.
Sunny smiled.
"Come. Today, I will make you regret being immortal…"
Brandishing his sword, he lunged forward.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2636.txt
content: Sunny had dismissed the Marvelous Mimic from the Forgotten Shore while four of his incarnations were inside, and then summoned it to the Eternal City. That was how five of his avatars ended up at the bottom of the Stormsea at the same time.

He called upon the Shadow Legion and summoned the Puppeteer to gain a flawless measure of control over his shades. Mind Weave allowed him to issue countless commands at the same time, so he could control all of his shades individually… but at the end of the day, he was still issuing orders.

With the great Moth controlling the shades like marionettes, though, Sunny could do more. Instead of giving endless commands to make his shades do what he wanted them to, he only needed to command one — and as long as an incarnation of his was wrapped around the Puppeteer, the entire Shadow Legion became no different from an extension of his body.

That meant that he was not limited to giving orders anymore, he could control his shades like he did his body. The cohesion of the Shadow Legion was therefore nearly flawless now, and more than that, every shade — even those of mindless beasts — could fight with the same skill and technique Sunny possessed.
Needless to say, his mastery of Shadow Dance was instrumental in controlling thousands of varied bodies skillfully.
Armed with these advantages, Sunny engaged the fallen immortals of the Eternal City in battle. Their crushing collective Will washed over him, and was repelled by his own with the help of the Curse.
But that was merely the boring part… the necessary part.
Now, however, was his time to have fun.
'No, really, what is wrong with me? Is this my idea of fun?'
Encased in the fearsome carapace of the Jade Mantle, wielding the Soul Serpent, and empowered by the Curse, Sunny plunged into the swarm of grotesque, immortal abominations.
His shield was broken under the avalanche of fleshy tendrils, but instantly restored itself as Sunny manifested more shadows into its shape.
His slaying blade cut the fallen immortals down one after another, but failed to kill them.
His armor remained unscathed, for now, both protecting his body and empowering it through the Curse.

Sunny struggled a little with not getting drunk on his fierce strength. In that moment, he truly felt like a demigod — his power had transcended the mundane, forcing the world to bend and change shape around his movements.

Of course, there was a wall standing in his way. That wall was the Will of his enemies… but while Sunny could not demolish it completely, he could overcome it enough to not feel too restrained. The Will technique he had been developing for a long while now helped him to achieve that.
So, yes… he was having fun.
The battle was a little exhilarating.

It had been a long time since he used that much of his power, and even more since he had been matched this well against an enemy. His legion was battling the mob of abominable immortals all around him, and he was barely staying alive while cutting down his foes — it was in that narrow gap between triumph and the cold defeat of death that the thrill of a battle lay.
Best of all, his enemies were immortal. So, he could kill them as many times as he wanted.
Naturally… the fact that they were immortal was also infinitely troubling.
'What exactly must I do here?'

Deflecting a lightning-fast tendril of repugnant flesh with his shield, Sunny pierced the body of a fallen immortal and sliced it apart. A split second later, he was already moving to avoid the next lethal strike while raising his sword to deliver a swift downward slash.

He was doing well in the battle against the fallen immortals, and the Shadow Legion was confidently overcoming them as well. But these were eternal beings, after all — no matter how many times they were destroyed, they would endlessly rise from the dead. If nothing changed, the scales of battle would begin to favor them soon, and eventually, they would swallow both him and his shades.
He needed to find a way to contain them.

The first idea Sunny had was to feed the undying fiends to either Fiend or Mimic. However, he was wary of that method since it could poison and destroy his Shadows… with how indestructible these abominations were, there was no telling what would happen if one of them was consumed.
Luckily, there were other potential solutions, as well.

The simplest one was to toss the fallen immortals into the Shadow Realm. After all, immortality was not an inherent trait of these fiends — it was the Eternal City that kept reviving them. So, moving them to somewhere else was supposed to solve the problem, especially if that place was the Realm of Death.

However, there were two problems with this idea. First, Sunny would have to drag every abomination into the Shadow Lantern personally, by pulling them into the shadows. Second, he was not sure that it was even possible — something told Sunny that the barrier created by Daeron would prevent the fallen immortals from leaving the Eternal City, even in that unconventional way.
'What else, then?'

He could potentially use the Puppeteer to take the immortals under control. And yet, that was not guaranteed to work, either — after all, the great Moth was merely a shadow of the Spirit of Doubt. It did not possess a Will of its own, so subjugating creatures of the Great Rank was unlikely to work. Apart from that, the Eternal City could simply return the immortals to a state prior to subjugation.
'Well, then…'
Sunny continued to fight, dividing his mind between countless shades and his own body.
There was one way that could work, even if it demanded more effort to implement.
'Yes… yes, that should do the trick.'
He smiled darkly.
Before Sunny could subdue the fallen immortals in that way, though, they had to be thoroughly defeated.
The Shadow Legion had its work cut out for it.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2637.txt
content: A world-shaking roar thundered above the battlefield, and Sunny felt cold terror shackle his limbs like a vice. His movements slowed, and the foundation of his Will seemed to crumble.
Instantly, tendrils of flesh moved faster than lightning and shot past his defenses to pierce his limbs, jerking him in four different directions to pull his body apart.
Gritting his teeth, Sunny steeled his mind and shook off the mental attack that had weakened him… was it even a mental attack, if it affected his very spirit?... and strained his muscles.

Instead of being pulled apart, he overpowered the four immortals who were trying to quarter him into the dark embrace of shadows. There, his vast and shapeless form tore their abominable shadows apart, scattering them across the boundless darkness. A moment later, he assumed a physical shape once more, rising from the bloodied rubble with a cold expression on his hidden face.
The breached surface of the Jade Mantle was already repairing itself, and drops of crimson blood were falling from his sword.
'What was that roar?'

Casting his senses into the depths of the Eternal City, he felt a massive shadow barreling toward the plaza. Then, a second roar crashed into the Shadow Legion, and an enormous tiger with glistening silver fur and golden stripes leaped into the mass of fallen immortals, its bestial eyes shining with a beautiful glow.

In the next moment, the flesh on the back of the giant tiger rippled and bulged with repugnant growths, which then exploded into a forest of deadly tendrils. The tendrils shot forward, piercing countless shades and ripping them apart.
Sunny's gaze turned somber.
'So, the Eternal City was not populated by immortal humans alone…'
It seemed like there had been immortal creatures here, as well, and all of them were now twisted by Corruption.

The abominable beast plunged into the battle formation of the Shadow Legion, shattering it. With every leap, the graceful figure of the silver tiger grew more monstrous and misshapen, slowly transforming it into a nightmarish mass of slithering flesh.
Before the abominations could deal too much damage, however, an enormous hand forged of blackened metal rose from the darkness and grabbed its neck, slicing it open.

As a waterfall of blood poured to the ground, Fiend emerged from the shadows, raising the giant tiger into the air — the devastating tendrils scraped against his mental carapace without being able to pierce it, and as infernal flames ignited in his eyes, wisps of smoke escaped through the tears in the abomination's fur.
Sunny focused on repairing the battle formation of the Shadow Legion, feeling a little disappointed that he could not deal with the abominable tiger himself.
Well… he could, in theory. After all, the damn thing was just as immortal as its human kin — so, all he had to do was command Fiend to retreat.
But that would be a bit too unhinged a decision, even for him.
Raising his shield, Sunny moved forward.

Slowly at first, then faster and faster, the Shadow Legion gained an upper hand over the disorganized mob of fallen immortals. Despite all their great power, the denizens of the Eternal City seemed more like mindless beasts than people — so, they failed to overcome the flawless cohesion and discipline of the silent shades.
Of course, their inferior numbers played the decisive role.

Eventually, Sunny gained complete control of the battlefield. The grotesque creatures were still refusing to die, but at some point, he managed to divide and isolate them from each other. Once that happened, the losses of the Shadow Legion almost ceased.
When the fallen immortals were imprisoned in a ruthless loop of being killed, reviving, and immediately being slain again, he proceeded to the next step.
The actions of the shades changed.

Instead of cutting the fallen immortals down, the Shadow Legion attempted to restrain them. At first, Sunny failed to contain many of the abominations, causing more shades to be destroyed — containing a Nightmare Creature was much harder than killing it, after all.
But eventually, he succeeded.
Using the Puppeteer and his legion of shades, Sunny slowly managed to restrain each of the corrupted immortals.
That was how the first battle he fought in the Eternal City ended.

Sunny stood among the rubble, breathing heavily. The plaza around him was completely destroyed, and all the ornate buildings around it had turned to ruins. The Dark Castle towered behind him, casting a deep shadow on the scene of utter devastation.
Soon enough, though, the plaza returned to its spotless beauty, and the ornate buildings assembled themselves from the rubble.
The black waters of the Stormsea rolled far above his head, glistening with reflected light.
Dismissing his helmet, Sunny inhaled deeply and wiped the sweat off his brow. Jet appeared in a gust of cold wind, making him shiver.
She seemed unscathed, if a little tired.
'Ah, I am worried.'

Fighting an immortal opponent was already bad enough, but it was woefully worse for Jet — after all, she needed a constant influx of soul essence to stay alive. Since they could not really kill these abominations, she could not receive any essence either. All she could do was waste it without getting anything back.
Would Jet even survive a prolonged confrontation for the Eternal City?
Sunny sighed.
Looking around, at the silent figures of shades who were struggling to hold the abominable immortals down, Jet asked in a somber tone:
"What now?"
Sunny hesitated for a short while, then answered evenly:
"Now, we show these hellspawn creatures a different, far more cruel hell."
With that, his shadows flowed off his body to the ground, and then assumed their corporeal forms. The fifth incarnation stepped out of the shadows, as well, having left the Puppeteer.
There was the sound of adamantine hooves ringing on the stone, and Nightmare approached them, blood dripping from its steel fangs.
Sunny inhaled deeply, and then looked at his steed.
"Sing them a lullaby, Nightmare."
With that, all five of Sunny's avatars turned into shadows and wrapped themselves around the black stallion.
Nightmare's crimson eyes ignited with terrifying flames.

chapters.title: Shadow Slave_Chapter_2638.txt
content: The fallen immortals of the Eternal City could not be destroyed, but they had to be contained.
So, of all the measures available to Sunny, Nightmare and his [Dream Curse] seemed like the best choice.

[Dream Curse] Ability Description: "This Shadow is a master of a lesser dream domain. It is capable of lulling living beings to sleep and imprisoning their souls in a labyrinth of nightmares. The souls destroyed within the nightmare maze feed the Shadow; the more powerful the Shadow grows, the faster and further the dream curse spreads."
It was an insidious and terrifying power, made only more eerie by how closely it resembled some traits of the Nightmare Spell. It was also what made Nightmare a Terror.

No two Terrors were alike, but there were similarities between all of them. Just like Tyrants were usually distinguished by their ability to rule lesser creatures and control territories, Terrors were distinguished by their ability to influence the world and spread. They were more akin to dreadful phenomena than singular beings, and it was that escalating nature of their influence that made them so dangerous.

Curiously enough, most Titans did not seem to possess a similar kind of spreading influence, and while some were followed by swarms of Nightmare Creatures, they did not really rule or care to rule them. The abominations were simply drawn to them like crows that followed slaughtering armies, while Titans usually regarded them with indifferent disinterest.

They were aloof and solitary creatures that seemed to exist in a world of their own. Strangely, that seemed like a downgrade compared to the vast swarms of abominable minions that served Tyrants or the exponentially expanding influence of Terrors… and yet, nobody who had ever faced a Titan on a battlefield would think them weaker than beings of a lesser Class.

The process of Terrors evolving to Tians was shrouded in mystery and largely unknown, but there was a reason why becoming a Titan was the final and most difficult step that a creature could take on the path of evolution. There was also a reason why another, less frequently used word to describe Titans was Calamities…

That said, Titans were exceedingly rare. And while Terrors were rare as well, Sunny had suffered far more while encountering the few he did meet. Some of the most dreadful foes he had ever clashed with were Terrors: the Soul Devourer, the Terror of the Crimson Spire, the Terror of LO49, Skinwalker… that odious Thieving Bird…
And Nightmare.

The [Dream Curse] could spread like a plague, both consuming his prey and making the tenebrous steed stronger. It was insidiously effective, as well, capable of subduing even creatures that were far stronger than its source — that was how Sunny had collected an army of Great Nightmare Creatures in Godgrave and dropped a Cursed Demon on Mordret's head.

Most chillingly of all, the Dream Curse was subtle. Its victims did not even know that they were under attack until they were lulled to sleep and locked in an inescapable labyrinth of nightmares — and by then, it was already too late. That was the reason why Nightmare could prey on abominations of higher Ranks than him.
Even if the Dream Curse failed to lull a creature to sleep at first, he could continue to subtly influence them until their defenses were weakened, and they could not resist anymore.
However…
There was a limit even to that insidious power, of course.

Nightmare was merely a Transcendent Terror, after all. It had taken Sunny and him close to two years to gradually assemble the swarm of Great Nightmare Creatures in Godgrave, and it had not been an easy feat either.

The fallen immortals from this district of the Eternal City alone were greater in number than those abominations, and each of them was ferociously powerful. There would be many more before the expedition was over, and Sunny did not have years to slowly subdue them all… so, he was not entirely confident that Nightmare would be able to lull them all to sleep.

Even if he could, there was no guarantee that his steed would be able to contain these monsters in the maze of nightmares indefinitely. Sunny himself had escaped that maze once, after all, shattering its walls during his battle against Nightmare.

Those walls were made from the nightmares that served the black stallion. Nightmare planted their seeds into the hearts of living beings and then harvested them, turning them into his minions. The fallen immortals would endure quite a few, no doubt… but they would dream their own nightmares as well.
In the end, Nightmare would have to subjugate the dreams of ancient immortals faster than his minions were destroyed, building his own legion of nightmares dreadful enough to contain them.

Subduing the grotesque abominations and keeping them subdued — these were the two problems facing Sunny, and he was not sure that a Transcendent Terror could manage both, at least not in a timely manner.
But while Nightmare had not evolved to a new Rank since Godgrave, Sunny had.
He had grown stronger in many aspects apart from Rank, as well. And five of his incarnations were here.
With five Supreme shadows augmenting Nightmare, he had a good chance of solving at least the first problem — infecting the restrained immortals with the Dream Curse.
From that point onward, though, Nightmare would be on his own. No one but the dark destrier himself could keep his victims lost in the labyrinth of dreadful dreams.
'Come on, Nightmare…'

While countless shades struggled to restrain the fallen immortals, Nightmare lowered his head and cast his terrifying, crimson gaze at them. Sunny could not see the Dream Curse spreading, and he could not even sense it…
But the world around them suddenly felt dark and terrifying, and an eerie chill seemed to permeate the air.
Far above, perched atop the Dark Castle, the Puppeteer moved its antennae, as if wary of something.
Jet looked around with a slight frown, her beautiful face growing a little paler.
Time seemed to stretch, flowing slowly…
But then, eventually, there was a subtle change to the strange and eerie scene in front of them.

Just a few meters away from Nightmare, a beautiful youth was sprawled on the ground, held firmly in place by black chains and the mandibles of several Obsidian Wasps. Despite his calm and benevolent expression, the immortal was straining to free himself forcefully, the stone around him cracking and mending itself without stop.

Gradually, though, his struggles seemed to grow less intense. His gaze grew dimmer, and his eyelids grew heavy. The young man blinked a few times, his eyes remaining closer for a bit longer each time.
And then, they did not open.
His struggles ceased.
He was asleep.
Sunny let out a relieved sigh.
'It seems to be working.'
After that fist success, many more followed, as well. Soon enough, an eerie silence enveloped the plaza.

The shades were standing without movement, and on the ground between them, the harrowing immortals of the Eternal City were sleeping peacefully, the black currents of the Stormsea glistening with silver light in the sky above them.
…Or maybe not so peacefully, at all.
